blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How come you never talked to me before? Like this.
I didn't plan to ask that. It came out like something that had been sitting there waiting.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Because you didn't need it before.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I've had hard weeks before this.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know. But those were hard in a way you could handle inside. This is — different. This is about who you are when somebody who loves you is pushing you in the wrong direction.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He wasn't wrong though. About any of it.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He was right that I needed a push. Right that I wait too long. Right that I get in my own way.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: All that can be true and what you said to him can still be right.
I looked at him.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You didn't tell him he was wrong. You told him you needed space to be wrong yourself. That's a different thing, Larry.
Nobody had ever called me by my name like that — not differently, just — exactly right. Like the name was the point. Like he wanted me to hear it.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't want to lose him over this.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I don't think you will. But even if things are strange for a while — that's allowed. You're not responsible for him not understanding you yet. You just said a true thing.
We sat a little longer. The street did its Saturday morning thing around us. Nobody said anything else.
Eventually Mr. Cole stood up.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Come eat something. Your brother's been hovering since eight.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: He has?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: He made eggs.
I got up.
